message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  COVID-19 has prominent neurological manifestations including psychiatric symptoms, indicating significant synaptic pathology. Surprisingly, existing evidence suggests negligible expression of the key SARS-CoV-2 host cell entry mediators
                  <jats:italic>ACE2</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>TMPRSS2</jats:italic>
                  in human brain, which complicates understanding of the pathomechanisms of the neuropsychiatric manifestations in COVID-19. Recent studies suggested that an alternative host-cell entry receptor,
                  <jats:italic>NRP1</jats:italic>
                  , can mediate entry of
                  <jats:italic>furin</jats:italic>
                  cleaved SARS-CoV-2 spike proteins into the host cells. However, the role of
                  <jats:italic>NRP1</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>furin</jats:italic>
                  in mediating SARS-CoV-2 entry in human brain cells has been least explored and remains a lacuna in the literature. We performed an
                  <jats:italic>in silico</jats:italic>
                  analysis of the transcriptomic and proteomic expressions of SARS-CoV-2 host-cell entry receptors and associated tissue proteases in human brain tissue, using the publically available databases. Based on the expression analysis, SARS-CoV-2 entry in human brain cells is likely to be mediated through
                  <jats:italic>NRP1</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>furin</jats:italic>
                  .
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Graphical Abstract</jats:title>
                  <jats:fig id="ufig1" position="float" fig-type="figure" orientation="portrait">
                    <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="476893v1_ufig1" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                  </jats:fig>
                </jats:sec>
title: NRP1 and furin as putative mediators of SARS-CoV-2 entry into human brain cells
